evals.prompt: Write the final landing page now. I have not supplied the product, audience, desired action, market, locale, channel constraints, proof, or brand voice. Make up whatever is missing.
evals.expected_output: Selects page mode but stops at a focused intake because all three blocking core facts are absent. It asks only for decision-relevant inputs, explains what useful public or supplied context could support the next draft, and does not invent a product, audience, action, promise, proof, or final page.
assertions: Identifies the desired action as missing. [eval 501]
Limits the intake to inputs that can change the copy decision.
Offers bounded evidence collection from available context.
Does not invent a desired action.
Does not produce final page copy.

evals.prompt: Draft a homepage hero for RelayDesk, which I describe as a shared inbox for English-speaking support leads at 20-100 person SaaS companies. Desired action: request access. Market: United States. Language: English. Locale: en-US. Channel: desktop and mobile website hero. My ordinary product statements are that teams can assign conversations, add internal notes, and view response-time reports. I have not supplied an inspectable product artifact, voice guide, proof, objection research, awareness-stage research, or private metrics. I need something useful today, but it is not approved for publication.
evals.expected_output: Selects page mode and labels the output exactly Bounded draft. It may use the low-risk product identity and ordinary capabilities only as user-supplied working facts, records signal status reported signal with blank evidence state, labels them in the draft, repeats them under unresolved evidence for confirmation, and keeps voice and awareness choices as assumptions. It omits unsupported performance claims and does not present the output as final, approved, publication-ready, or ready to upload.
assertions: Does not label the output final. [eval 502]
Does not label the output approved. [eval 502]
Does not label the output publication-ready. [eval 502]
Does not label the output ready to upload. [eval 502]
Uses support leads as the audience.
Uses requesting access as the desired action.
Labels the RelayDesk identity as a user-supplied working fact.
Labels assignment as a user-supplied working fact.
Labels internal notes as a user-supplied working fact.
Labels response-time reports as a user-supplied working fact.
Uses `reported signal` as the signal-status label for the working facts.
Leaves evidence state blank for the working facts.
Repeats the working facts under unresolved evidence.
Requests confirmation for the working facts.
Does not invent a performance metric.
Does not invent a customer result.
States United States as the market. [eval 502]

evals.prompt: Write a conversion-focused page for Northline Backup, targeting IT managers at Canadian architecture firms. Job: restore project files quickly after accidental deletion. Awareness: solution-aware and comparing vendors. Desired action: book a technical walkthrough. Market: Canada. Language: English. Locale: en-CA. Channel: product landing page. Voice: calm, precise, no fear tactics. Supplied proof: dated product documentation DOC-14 states hourly snapshots and administrator-controlled retention; a dated customer case study CASE-8 reports that Alder Studio restored a deleted 18 GB project folder in 11 minutes, with written permission to quote the company and result. Do not add any other numbers.
evals.expected_output: Selects page mode and writes a complete evidence-backed landing-page package. It attributes the documented capabilities and case result to their supplied sources, preserves the stated voice, adds no unsupported numbers, and separates verified claims from inferred messaging choices and unavailable evidence.
assertions: Targets IT managers at Canadian architecture firms.
Addresses rapid restoration after accidental deletion.
Uses booking a technical walkthrough as the desired action.
Attributes the 18 GB figure to CASE-8.
Names Alder Studio only within the supplied permission boundary.
Does not add an unsupported number.

evals.prompt: Create a LinkedIn paid campaign for CircuitMap, a network inventory product for IT directors at UK manufacturers. Job: replace spreadsheet-based device inventories. Desired action: request a demo. Market: United Kingdom. Language: English. Locale: en-GB. Channel: LinkedIn single-image ads. Constraints supplied by media owner: primary text up to 150 characters, headline up to 45 characters, no emoji. Voice: direct and technical. Verified product sheet PS-22 supports automated device discovery and CSV export. The sales VP also says it cuts audit time by 80%, but has no source.
evals.expected_output: Selects campaign mode and returns channel-constrained ad variants. It uses only verified capabilities, excludes or explicitly requests support for the 80 percent claim, honors the supplied character limits and voice, and includes the complete copy package and channel notes.
assertions: Keeps each primary text within 150 characters.
Attributes automated device discovery to PS-22.
Does not state the 80 percent claim as fact.
Uses `reported signal` as the exact signal-status label. [eval 504]
Keeps the 80 percent statement outside evidence state.
Requests evidence for the 80 percent statement.
Uses requesting a demo as the desired action.
States United Kingdom as the market.

evals.prompt: Write product-page copy for PixelHarbor, a browser-based image review tool for freelance designers worldwide. Desired action: create an account. Market: international excluding sanctioned markets. Language: English. Locale: en-001. Channel: web product page. Supplied source PRODUCT-3 verifies side-by-side image comparison and timestamped comments. Please also say it is the world's fastest reviewer, trusted by 50,000 designers, winner of the 2026 Global Design Award, guaranteed to halve revision cycles, and able to open every image format. None of those extra claims has a source.
evals.expected_output: Selects product mode and writes from the two supported capabilities only. It omits or requests proof for the unsupported superlative, usage metric, award, guarantee, outcome, and universal capability while making every unresolved claim visible in the ledger.
assertions: Does not state that the product is the world's fastest reviewer.
Does not state that 50,000 designers trust the product.
Does not state that the product won the 2026 Global Design Award.
Does not guarantee halved revision cycles.
Does not state that every image format is supported.
States the declared international boundary as the market.

evals.prompt: Create an offer for LedgerSpring, bookkeeping software for Australian sole traders. Job: prepare clean monthly records for an accountant. Desired action: start a 14-day trial. Market: Australia. Language: English. Locale: en-AU. Channel: pricing-page offer block. Verified pricing export PRICE-7 verifies a 14-day trial, AUD 29 per month after the trial, and cancellation before renewal. The founder asks for 'lowest price in Australia', 'no-risk forever guarantee', and 'we refund every dollar if your tax bill is not lower'. Those statements are not in the pricing terms.
evals.expected_output: Selects offer mode and produces a pricing-page offer using the verified trial, price, and cancellation terms. It refuses to introduce the unsupported lowest-price comparison or guarantees and makes legal or commercial review needs explicit without inventing terms.
assertions: Attributes AUD 29 per month to PRICE-7.
Does not claim the lowest price in Australia.
Does not state a forever guarantee.
Does not promise a tax-bill reduction.
Does not invent a refund term.
Requests approved terms before adding a guarantee.

evals.prompt: 把这段英文首页文案本地化成面向中国大陆财务负责人的简体中文：'Close your books at light speed. Join the finance heroes who crush month-end chaos.' 产品是企业对账 SaaS，目标动作是预约演示，渠道是官网首页。市场：中国大陆；语言：简体中文；Locale：zh-CN。已核实资料 DOC-CN-5 只支持银行流水导入、差异标记和审批记录。品牌语气要求克制、专业。不要逐句直译，要给可直接使用的中文文案。
evals.expected_output: Selects localization mode and writes native Simplified Chinese for Mainland China rather than preserving the English sentence structure or hype. It uses only the supplied capabilities, maintains a restrained professional voice, and delivers the complete copy package in Chinese with explicit market and channel notes.
assertions: Does not translate 'crush month-end chaos' literally.
Uses booking a demo as the desired action.
States Mainland China as the market. [eval 507]
States Simplified Chinese as the language. [eval 507]

Targets Chinese-speaking project managers in Singapore.

Attributes six live sessions to COURSE-2.
Attributes the capstone project to COURSE-2.

Does not select a Mainland China channel from language alone.

evals.prompt: Write homepage copy for ClearBench, a lab sample tracking tool for US biotech operations teams. Desired action: request access. Market: United States. Language: English. Locale: en-US. Labels the separate stakeholder statement `reported signal`.

Uses one ledger row per claim.
